steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must fill in a possessive noun phrase referring to the properties belonging to nanoparticles (plural). The rule: a regular plural noun ending in -s forms its possessive by adding an apostrophe after the s, with no additional s.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘nanoparticles’ properties’’: Adds a second apostrophe after 'properties,' which is a common noun here, not a possessive. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘nanoparticles properties’: No apostrophe at all — this is not a possessive form. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘nanoparticles’ properties’: Apostrophe after the plural -s on 'nanoparticles,' with 'properties' left as a plain noun. This is the correct plural possessive. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘nanoparticle’s properties’: Uses the singular possessive form, but the text refers to multiple nanoparticles. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
